channel.title: Posts on Tinkering
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Tinkering

item.title: Bringing async to serial devices
item.description: I’m an experimental physicist, so part of my job entails writing software that orchestrates equipment in my experiment. Most of the code that runs my experiment is written in a proprietary scripting language that I guarantee none of you have ever heard of. The old code is brittle, difficult to debug, and generally makes it too much of a pain to add features unless absolutely necessary. Lately I’ve been thinking about how I would do a modernized rewrite in Python.

item.title: Introduction to Procedural Macros in Rust
item.description: As a newcomer to Rust, I heard the phrase “procedural macro” thrown around a lot without really understanding what it meant. I figured that I would learn about them if I ever needed them. Well, I’m working on the guts of relm, and a large chunk of it is procedural macros. I’ve learned enough about procedural macros to be dangerous, so I thought I would pass on some knowledge.
